options.name: HOST_LC_ALL
options.description: Set value to a locale to override all other system locale settings for a game.  This variable should be used instead of LC_ALL.

options.name: LICENSE
options.description: See the LICENSE files here, as well as the LICENSE, COPYING, etc files in each submodule and directory for details. If you distribute a built version of Proton to other users, you must adhere to the terms of these licenses.

options.name: PROTON_CRASH_REPORT_DIR
options.description: Write crash logs into this directory. Does not clean up old logs, so may eat all your disk space eventually.

options.name: PROTON_DISABLE_NVAPI
options.description: Disable NVIDIA's NVAPI GPU support library.

options.name: PROTON_DXVK_D3D8
options.description: Use DXVK's d3d8.dll.

options.name: PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE
options.description: Force Wine to enable the LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE flag for all executables. Enabled by default.

options.name: PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE
options.description: Delay freeing some memory, to work around application use-after-free bugs.

options.name: PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU
options.description: Force Nvidia GPUs to always be reported as AMD GPUs. Some games require this if they depend on Windows-only Nvidia driver functionality. See also DXVK's nvapiHack config, which only affects reporting from Direct3D.

options.name: PROTON_LOG
options.description: Convenience method for dumping a useful debug log to $PROTON_LOG_DIR/steam-$APPID.log. Set to 1 to enable default logging, or set to a string to be appended to the default WINEDEBUG channels.

options.name: PROTON_LOG_DIR
options.description: Output log files into the directory specified. Defaults to your home directory.

options.name: PROTON_NO_D3D10
options.description: Disable d3d10.dll and dxgi.dll, for d3d10 games which can fall back to and run better with d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_NO_D3D11
options.description: Disable d3d11.dll, for d3d11 games which can fall back to and run better with d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_NO_ESYNC
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 11.0. In older versions, do not use eventfd-based in-process synchronization primitives.

options.name: PROTON_NO_FSYNC
options.description: Do not use futex-based in-process synchronization primitives. (Automatically disabled on systems with no FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE support.)

options.name: PROTON_NO_NTSYNC
options.description: Do not use ntsync.

options.name: PROTON_OLD_GL_STRING
options.description: Set some driver overrides to limit the length of the GL extension string, for old games that crash on very long extension strings.

options.name: PROTON_SET_GAME_DRIVE
options.description: Create an S: drive which points to the Steam Library which contains the game.

options.name: PROTON_USE_D9VK
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 5.0. In older versions, use Vulkan-based DXVK instead of OpenGL-based wined3d for d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_USE_SECCOMP
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 5.13. In older versions, enable seccomp-bpf filter to emulate native syscalls, required for some DRM protections to work.

options.name: PROTON_USE_WINED3D
options.description: Use OpenGL-based wined3d instead of Vulkan-based DXVK for d3d11, d3d10, and d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_USE_XALIA
options.description: Enable Xalia, a program that can add a gamepad UI for some keyboard/mouse interfaces, or set to 0 to disable. The default is to enable it dynamically based on window contents.

options.name: PROTON_WAIT_ATTACH
options.description: Wait for a debugger to attach to steam.exe before launching the game process. To attach to the game process at startup, debuggers should be set to follow child processes.

options.name: STEAM_COMPAT_CONFIG
options.description: some options for known games using the STEAM_COMPAT_CONFIG variable. You can override these options using the environment variables described below. The best way to set these environment overrides for all games is by renaming user_settings.sample.py to user_settings.py and modifying it appropriately. This file is located in the Proton installation directory in your Steam library

options.name: VKD3D_CONFIG
options.description: Put force_bindless_texel_buffer into VKD3D_CONFIG.

options.name: WINE_DISABLE_VULKAN_OPWR
options.description: Enable hack to disable Vulkan other process window rendering which sometimes causes issues on Wayland due to blit being one frame behind.

options.name: WINE_DO_NOT_CREATE_DXGI_DEVICE_MANAGER
options.description: Enable hack to work around video issues in some games due to incomplete IMFDXGIDeviceManager support.

options.name: WINE_FULLSCREEN_INTEGER_SCALING
options.description: Enable integer scaling mode, to give sharp pixels when upscaling.

options.name: WINE_USE_KWIN_HACKS
options.description: Enable KDE-specific windowing hacks that may improve experience with KDE older than 6.4 on Wayland and KDE older than 6.6 on X11.

options.name: DXVK_HDR
options.description: Enables HDR when set to DXVK_HDR=1, optionally combined with ENABLE_HDR_WSI=1 if required, on Nvidia driver versions older than 595.x.x.

options.name: DXVK_NO_HDR
options.description: The opposite of DXVK_HDR=1, forcefully disables HDR if it is automatically enabled. NOTE: Presently Gnome does not support enough color management features to expose HDR automatically.

options.name: DXVK_NVAPI_VKREFLEX
options.description: Enables DXVK-NVAPI's Vulkan Reflex layer to support Reflex in Vulkan games. It's meant to enable Reflex in games such as Portal RTX, Path of Exile 1 and 2 and Doom TDA, etc.

options.name: HOST_LC_ALL
options.description: Set value to a locale to override all other system locale settings for a game.  This variable should be used instead of LC_ALL.

options.name: LOW_LATENCY_LAYER
options.description: Enables low_latency_layer, for more information and and configuration options refer to the projects README.

options.name: PIPEWIRE_LATENCY
options.description: Setting PIPEWIRE_LATENCY does nothing here, use the quantum above. * Choose output device - games follow your PipeWire default sink. Change it in your sound settings, pavucontrol (Playback tab), or wpctl set-default . The game shows up named after itself or as winepipewire. * You can make sure the game uses winepipewire.drv either through pw-top or by looking at the audio device names in the game itself which should include (Pipewire) in their name.

options.name: PROTON_CRASH_REPORT_DIR
options.description: Write crash logs into this directory. Does not clean up old logs, so may eat all your disk space eventually.

options.name: PROTON_D7VK_DDRAW
options.description: Use ddraw.dll from the D7VK project for DirectX 7-or-lower games.

options.name: PROTON_DISABLE_NVAPI
options.description: Disable NVIDIA's NVAPI GPU support library.

options.name: PROTON_DISCORD_BRIDGE
options.description: Enables rpc-bridge to allow games running in Proton to set Discord's Rich Presence.

options.name: PROTON_DLSS_INDICATOR
options.description: Enable the DLSS overlay at the bottom left portion of the screen.

options.name: PROTON_DLSS_UPGRADE
options.description: g).dll DLLs. Version to download can be specified by supplying it as a value, like so PROTON_DLSS_UPGRADE="310.2", instead of 1, to download version 310.2.1.0. This option also sets DXVK_NVAPI_DRS_SETTINGS` to use the latest preset. If you provide your own configuration through this environment variable, your configuration will override the defaults.

options.name: PROTON_DXVK_D3D8
options.description: Use DXVK's d3d8.dll.

options.name: PROTON_DXVK_LOWLATENCY
options.description: Enable the alternative dxvk-low-latency fork, which enhances the original dxvk with low-latency frame pacing capabilities to improve game responsiveness and input lag. It also improves latency stability over time, usually resulting in a more accurate playback speed of the generated video. Refer to the project's documentation for in-depth configuration.

options.name: PROTON_DXVK_SAREK
options.description: Use the dxvk-sarek fork as DXVK replacement for older GPUs that don't properly support Vulkan 1.3 (supported Vulkan 1.1.x to 1.2.x). It is using the async branch, so it SHOULD NOT to be used with games using anti-cheat or multiplayer games in general. You have been warned.

options.name: PROTON_ENABLE_HDR
options.description: DXVK_HDR

options.name: PROTON_ENABLE_MEDIACONV
options.description: For debugging purposes, do not use.

options.name: PROTON_ENABLE_WAYLAND
options.description: Enable Wayland support. winewayland is experimental and work-in-progress, expect issues. When reporting issues ALWAYS mention if you using this environment variable, and make sure the issue still happens without it. Valve's Protons do not include winewayland.drv, they always use winex11.drv. For more information refer to this section

options.name: PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE
options.description: Force Wine to enable the LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE flag for all executables. Enabled by default.

options.name: PROTON_FSR4_INDICATOR
options.description: Enable the FSR4 watermark at the top left portion of the screen. This is a shorthand option for setting FSR_WATERMARK=1 and FSR_FG_WATERMARK=1.

options.name: PROTON_FSR4_UPGRADE
options.description: Automatically download amdxcffx64.dll and upgrade games with FSR 3.1 to use FSR 4. Version to download can be specified by supplying it as a value, like so PROTON_FSR4_UPGRADE="4.0.1", instead of 1. Downloads the latest available and known-good version of the required DLL by default.

options.name: PROTON_GST_VIDEO_ORIENTATION
options.description: Change the orientation (rotation) of videos rendered using gstreamer. The accepted values are the same as gstreamer's videoflip plugin

options.name: PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE
options.description: Delay freeing some memory, to work around application use-after-free bugs.

options.name: PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU
options.description: Force Nvidia GPUs to always be reported as AMD GPUs. Some games require this if they depend on Windows-only Nvidia driver functionality. See also DXVK's nvapiHack config, which only affects reporting from Direct3D.

options.name: PROTON_LOCAL_SHADER_CACHE
options.description: Enable per-game shader cache even if "Shader Pre-caching" is disabled. Any configuration set by this option can be overriden, i.e. if one of the environment variables is already set, the user-set value will be used. The default configuration is set up to mimic Steam's "Shader Pre-Caching", with shaders being cached under /shadercache/ for each game.

options.name: PROTON_LOG
options.description: Convenience method for dumping a useful debug log to $PROTON_LOG_DIR/steam-$APPID.log. Set to 1 to enable default logging, or set to a string to be appended to the default WINEDEBUG channels.

options.name: PROTON_LOG_DIR
options.description: Output log files into the directory specified. Defaults to your home directory.

options.name: PROTON_MEDIA_FORCE_GST
options.description: Fixes video/audio playback in cutscenes in some cases.

options.name: PROTON_MEDIA_USE_GST
options.description: Fixes video/audio playback in cutscenes in some cases.

options.name: PROTON_MLFG_UPGRADE
options.description: Enables the use of MLFG with FSR4 >= 4.0.3.

options.name: PROTON_NO_D3D10
options.description: Disable d3d10.dll and dxgi.dll, for d3d10 games which can fall back to and run better with d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_NO_D3D11
options.description: Disable d3d11.dll, for d3d11 games which can fall back to and run better with d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_NO_FSYNC
options.description: Do not use futex-based in-process synchronization primitives. (Automatically disabled on systems with no FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE support.)

options.name: PROTON_NO_WM_DECORATION
options.description: Disable window decorations by the the window manager, use wine's own window decorations.

options.name: PROTON_NVIDIA_LIBS
options.description: Enable alternative implementations of Nvidia libraries missing from Proton. Allows things like hardware-accelerated PhysX to function and should be use only when needed. This option enables all of nvcuda, nvenc, nvml and nvoptix. These are incompatible with wow64 and thus disabled when PROTON_USE_WOW64=1 is set.

options.name: PROTON_NVIDIA_LIBS_NO_32BIT
options.description: Use with the previous option, to enable only the 64bit nvidia libraries. Useful if you are using RTX series 4000 or 5000 GPUs and you had bad performance or crashes when enabling these with 32bit games.

options.name: PROTON_NVIDIA_NVCUDA
options.description: Enable alternative nvcuda.dll from nvidia-libs only.

options.name: PROTON_NVIDIA_NVENC
options.description: Enable alternative nvcencodeapi(64).dll from nvidia-libs only.

options.name: PROTON_NVIDIA_NVML
options.description: Enable alternative nvml.dll from nvidia-libs only. This option is enabled by default.

options.name: PROTON_NVIDIA_NVOPTIX
options.description: Enable alternative nvoptix.dll from nvidia-libs only.

options.name: PROTON_OLD_GL_STRING
options.description: Set some driver overrides to limit the length of the GL extension string, for old games that crash on very long extension strings.

options.name: PROTON_OPTISCALER_CONFIG
options.description: * You can use PROTON_OPTISCALER_CONFIG write arbitrary configuration in OptiScaler.ini. The config is a semicolon ( ;) separated string of individual options. The template for each option is {section}.{option}={value}, for example PROTON_OPTISCALER_CONFIG="Upscalers.Dx11Upscaler=fsr31;Upscalers.Dx12Upscaler=dlss". To avoid mistakes, only config options that already exist in Optiscaler.ini can be modified. * The changes to the config are permanent until OptiScaler is upgraded or downgraded to a different version. To modify a setting it has to either be manually edited or altered by applying a different config through the environment

options.name: PROTON_OPTISCALER_NAME
options.description: control the DLL that should be injected. Three names are supported for PROTON_OPTISCALER_NAME, dxgi.dll, d3d12.dll and dbghelp.dll and defaults to dxgi.dll unless explictly set. * You can use PROTON_OPTISCALER_CONFIG write arbitrary configuration in OptiScaler.ini. The config is a semicolon ( ;) separated string of individual options. The template for each option is {section}.{option}={value}, for example PROTON_OPTISCALER_CONFIG="Upscalers.Dx11Upscaler=fsr31;Upscalers.Dx12Upscaler=dlss". To avoid mistakes, only config options that already exist in Optiscaler.ini can be modified.

options.name: PROTON_PREFER_SDL
options.description: Uses SDL input instead of HIDRAW/Steam Input

options.name: PROTON_SET_GAME_DRIVE
options.description: Create an S: drive which points to the Steam Library which contains the game.

options.name: PROTON_USE_D9VK
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 5.0. In older versions, use Vulkan-based DXVK instead of OpenGL-based wined3d for d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_USE_OPTISCALER
options.description: Enables automatic OptiScaler injection. For more information refer to this section

options.name: PROTON_USE_PIPEWIRE
options.description: Enable the winepipewire.drv audio driver. This audio driver is enabled by default and can be disabled with PROTON_USE_PIPEWIRE=0. For more information and further configuration refer to this section

options.name: PROTON_USE_SDL
options.description: Alias of PROTON_PREFER_SDL

options.name: PROTON_USE_SECCOMP
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 5.13. In older versions, enable seccomp-bpf filter to emulate native syscalls, required for some DRM protections to work.

options.name: PROTON_USE_WAYLAND
options.description: Alias of PROTON_ENABLE_WAYLAND

options.name: PROTON_USE_WINED3D
options.description: Use OpenGL-based wined3d instead of Vulkan-based DXVK for d3d11, d3d10, and d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_USE_XALIA
options.description: Enable Xalia, a program that can add a gamepad UI for some keyboard/mouse interfaces, or set to 0 to disable. The default is to enable it dynamically based on window contents.

options.name: PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP
options.description: N/A

options.name: PROTON_VKREFLEX
options.description: Enables DXVK-NVAPI's Vulkan Reflex layer to support Reflex in Vulkan games. It's meant to enable Reflex in games such as Portal RTX, Path of Exile 1 and 2 and Doom TDA, etc.

options.name: PROTON_WAIT_ATTACH
options.description: Wait for a debugger to attach to steam.exe before launching the game process. To attach to the game process at startup, debuggers should be set to follow child processes.

options.name: STEAM_COMPAT_CONFIG
options.description: some options for known games using the STEAM_COMPAT_CONFIG variable. You can override these options using the environment variables described below. The best way to set these environment overrides for all games is by renaming user_settings.sample.py to user_settings.py and modifying it appropriately. This file is located in the Proton installation directory in your Steam library

options.name: VKD3D_CONFIG
options.description: Put force_bindless_texel_buffer into VKD3D_CONFIG.

options.name: WINE_AUDIO_DRIVER
options.description: Comma-separated list of audio drivers for wine to choose from. The default list is pipewire,pulse,alsa. For example WINE_AUDIO_DRIVER=pulse will use winepulse.drv exclusively.

options.name: WINE_BLOCK_HOSTS
options.description: Provides a list of hosts for Wine to not connect to. The list can be either comma (,) or semicolon (;) separated, i.e WINE_BLOCK_HOSTS=host1.org,host2.net. The maximum number of hosts is 16 and the maximum length of each host is 256 characters.

options.name: WINE_DISABLE_VULKAN_OPWR
options.description: Enable hack to disable Vulkan other process window rendering which sometimes causes issues on Wayland due to blit being one frame behind.

options.name: WINE_DO_NOT_CREATE_DXGI_DEVICE_MANAGER
options.description: Enable hack to work around video issues in some games due to incomplete IMFDXGIDeviceManager support.

options.name: WINE_FULLSCREEN_INTEGER_SCALING
options.description: Enable integer scaling mode, to give sharp pixels when upscaling.

options.name: WINE_USE_KWIN_HACKS
options.description: Enable KDE-specific windowing hacks that may improve experience with KDE older than 6.4 on Wayland and KDE older than 6.6 on X11.

options.name: WINEALSA_CHANNELS
options.description: Allows to select more than two channels with the winealsa driver as well as force disable spatial audio in games. Can be useful with surround setups or when the sounds come out wrong positionally (like dialogue coming out only from the left speaker). Possible values is the number of speakers, such as 2 (to disable spatial audio), such as 4 (2 front, 2 rear), 6 (5.1) or 8 (7.1).

options.name: WINEALSA_SPATIAL
options.description: Properly downmixes spatial with winealsa and includes the height channels. Use 1 to enable, 0 to disable (default). Recommended only if there are spatial audio errors with the WINEALSA_CHANNELS variable

options.name: WINEPULSE_FAST_POLLING
options.description: Can help eliminate crackling with the default audio driver. Use 1 to enable, 0 to disable (default). Can cause rare crackling in God of War Ragnarok with the power of two quants (e.g. 512/768/1024)

options.name: FSR_WATERMARK
options.description: Enables watermarks for FSR Upscaling and FG. FG watermark is incorrect on 4.1.1 (reports MLFG even when FP8 is not supported). Upscaling watermark works correctly. You can use this to determine which FSR3/4 type you are using (I8 or FP8)

options.name: FSR4_UPGRADE
options.description: Upgrade FSR3/4 to newer FSR4 (FP8 or I8) using AMD's FSR 4.1.1 amdxcffx64.dll taken from Proton Experimental. This option is not needed on RDNA2-4 discrete GPUs

options.name: MLFG_UPGRADE
options.description: Enables FSR4 MLFG upgrade to use redstone frame generation. Can be used in tandom with FSR4-I8 on RDNA3 using DXIL_SPIRV_CONFIG=wmma_rdna3_workaround.

options.name: PROTON_EMULATE_STEAMINPUT
options.description: Emulate Steam Input hooks with SDL input. This does not emulate the ISteamInput interface used by a handful of games on Steam (https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/api/isteaminput). Emulation for that interface is beyond my scope, since it would just be easier to hack steam overlay to make it work on wayland native apps.

options.name: PROTON_ENABLE_WAYLAND
options.description: Enables the winewayland driver

options.name: PROTON_USE_SDL
options.description: Uses SDL2/3 for gamepad input instead of steam input

options.name: WAYLANDDRV_PRIMARY_MONITOR
options.description: Tell the wayland driver what the primary monitor name is (Example: eDP-1). This is a workaround for a missing Wayland Protocol.

options.name: FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE
options.description: Do not use futex-based in-process synchronization primitives. (Automatically disabled on systems with no FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE support.)

options.name: LC_ALL
options.description: Set value to a locale to override all other system locale settings for a game.  This variable should be used instead of LC_ALL.

options.name: PROTON_DLSS_INDICATOR
options.description: Enable the DLSS overlay at the bottom left portion of the screen. This is exactly the same as FSR4_WATERMARK=1

options.name: PROTON_DLSS_UPGRADE
options.description: g).dll DLLs. Version to download can be specified by supplying it as a value, like so PROTON_DLSS_UPGRADE="310.2", instead of 1, to download version 310.2.1.0. This option also sets DXVK_NVAPI_DRS_SETTINGS` to use the latest preset. If you provide your own config for it through this environment variable, your configuration is going to be applied..

options.name: PROTON_ENABLE_MEDIACONV
options.description: Enable media converter for winegstreamer. This is not needed for winedmo, since the mediaconverter implementation of the codecs doesn't override the underlying implementation.

options.name: PROTON_FSR4_INDICATOR
options.description: Enable the FSR4 watermark at the top left portion of the screen.

options.name: PROTON_FSR4_RDNA3_UPGRADE
options.description: Identical to PROTON_FSR4_UPGRADE but for RDNA3 GPUs. Enables some required compatibility options and downloads version 4.0.0 of the DLL by default.

options.name: PROTON_FSR4_UPGRADE
options.description: Automatically download amdxcffx64.dll and upgrade games with FSR 3.1 to use FSR 4. Version to download can be specified by supplying it as a value, like so PROTON_FSR4_UPGRADE="4.0.1", instead of 1. Downloads version 4.0.2 of the required DLL by default. This option also disables AMD Anti-Lag 2 currently due to various issues.

options.name: PROTON_USE_SDL
options.description: Uses SDL input instead of HIDRAW/Steam Input.

options.name: PROTON_USE_WAYLAND
options.description: Enables the Wayland driver.

options.name: PROTON_USE_WINED3D11
options.description: Use OpenGL-based wined3d instead of Vulkan-based DXVK for d3d11 and d3d10. This used to be called PROTON_USE_WINED3D11, which is now an alias for this same option.

options.name: PROTON_USE_WOW64
options.description: Enables wow64.

options.name: WAYLANDDRV_PRIMARY_MONITOR
options.description: Specify primary monitor where the value is something like eDP-1. Requires the Wayland driver.

options.name: WAYLANDDRV_RAWINPUT
options.description: A value of 0 disables unaccelerated input and uses accelerated input. Any positive real number (like 0.5) adjusts the sensitivity of rawinput. Requires the Wayland driver.

options.name: WINE_FULLSCREEN_FSR_STRENGTH
options.description: Enable AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution (FSR), use in conjunction with WINE_FULLSCREEN_FSR_STRENGTH. Only works in Vulkan games (DXVK and VKD3D-Proton included).

options.name: HOST_LC_ALL
options.description: Set value to a locale to override all other system locale settings for a game.  This variable should be used instead of LC_ALL.

options.name: LICENSE
options.description: See the LICENSE files here, as well as the LICENSE, COPYING, etc files in each submodule and directory for details. If you distribute a built version of Proton to other users, you must adhere to the terms of these licenses.

options.name: PROTON_CRASH_REPORT_DIR
options.description: Write crash logs into this directory. Does not clean up old logs, so may eat all your disk space eventually.

options.name: PROTON_DISABLE_NVAPI
options.description: Disable NVIDIA's NVAPI GPU support library.

options.name: PROTON_DXVK_D3D8
options.description: Use DXVK's d3d8.dll.

options.name: PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE
options.description: Force Wine to enable the LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE flag for all executables. Enabled by default.

options.name: PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE
options.description: Delay freeing some memory, to work around application use-after-free bugs.

options.name: PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU
options.description: Force Nvidia GPUs to always be reported as AMD GPUs. Some games require this if they depend on Windows-only Nvidia driver functionality. See also DXVK's nvapiHack config, which only affects reporting from Direct3D.

options.name: PROTON_LOG
options.description: Convenience method for dumping a useful debug log to $PROTON_LOG_DIR/steam-$APPID.log. Set to 1 to enable default logging, or set to a string to be appended to the default WINEDEBUG channels.

options.name: PROTON_LOG_DIR
options.description: Output log files into the directory specified. Defaults to your home directory.

options.name: PROTON_NO_D3D10
options.description: Disable d3d10.dll and dxgi.dll, for d3d10 games which can fall back to and run better with d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_NO_D3D11
options.description: Disable d3d11.dll, for d3d11 games which can fall back to and run better with d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_NO_ESYNC
options.description: Do not use eventfd-based in-process synchronization primitives.

options.name: PROTON_NO_FSYNC
options.description: Do not use futex-based in-process synchronization primitives. (Automatically disabled on systems with no FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE support.)

options.name: PROTON_OLD_GL_STRING
options.description: Set some driver overrides to limit the length of the GL extension string, for old games that crash on very long extension strings.

options.name: PROTON_SET_GAME_DRIVE
options.description: Create an S: drive which points to the Steam Library which contains the game.

options.name: PROTON_USE_D9VK
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 5.0. In older versions, use Vulkan-based DXVK instead of OpenGL-based wined3d for d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_USE_SECCOMP
options.description: Note: Obsoleted in Proton 5.13. In older versions, enable seccomp-bpf filter to emulate native syscalls, required for some DRM protections to work.

options.name: PROTON_USE_WINED3D
options.description: Use OpenGL-based wined3d instead of Vulkan-based DXVK for d3d11, d3d10, and d3d9.

options.name: PROTON_USE_XALIA
options.description: Enable Xalia, a program that can add a gamepad UI for some keyboard/mouse interfaces, or set to 0 to disable. The default is to enable it dynamically based on window contents.

options.name: PROTON_WAIT_ATTACH
options.description: Wait for a debugger to attach to steam.exe before launching the game process. To attach to the game process at startup, debuggers should be set to follow child processes.

options.name: STEAM_COMPAT_CONFIG
options.description: some options for known games using the STEAM_COMPAT_CONFIG variable. You can override these options using the environment variables described below. The best way to set these environment overrides for all games is by renaming user_settings.sample.py to user_settings.py and modifying it appropriately. This file is located in the Proton installation directory in your Steam library

options.name: VKD3D_CONFIG
options.description: Put force_bindless_texel_buffer into VKD3D_CONFIG.

options.name: WINE_DISABLE_VULKAN_OPWR
options.description: Enable hack to disable Vulkan other process window rendering which sometimes causes issues on Wayland due to blit being one frame behind.

options.name: WINE_DO_NOT_CREATE_DXGI_DEVICE_MANAGER
options.description: Enable hack to work around video issues in some games due to incomplete IMFDXGIDeviceManager support.

options.name: WINE_FULLSCREEN_INTEGER_SCALING
options.description: Enable integer scaling mode, to give sharp pixels when upscaling.
